description: Statistics card on a white background from the Department of Labor. At the top, a bold navy serif all-caps underlined headline reads 'THE BIDEN JOB MARKET,' with an italic subheading 'Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024.' The center holds an embedded FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis) bar chart with a legend: light-blue bars 'Employment Level - Native Born' and gray bars 'Employment Level - Foreign Born,' plotted month by month from Aug 2023 to Aug 2024, with the native-born bars frequently below the zero line and the foreign-born bars above it. The source line reads 'Source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED. Shaded areas indicate U.S. recessions' and 'fred.stlouisfed.org.' Below the chart, two large summary lines read: 'NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000' and 'FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000' (the figures in blue serif). A small attribution at the bottom reads 'Sources: BLS, Federal Reserve Bank, Fox News.' The tweet text reads 'While foreign-born labor made GAINS under Biden, native-born Americans LOST jobs.'
notable_text: THE BIDEN JOB MARKET | Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024 | NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000 | FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000
